text: Came here after the PAPEA spring reception and this izakaya was the perfect wind-down. The karaage is crispy and the sake selection is genuinely impressive. Will absolutely be bringing the PAPEA steering committee here for our fall dinner.
Best izakaya in the Pearl District, possibly the whole city. The yakitori skewers are perfectly charred and the small plates keep coming. Great spot for a long evening with friends.
Brought my Bāchan here on her birthday and she declared it the best ramen outside of Osaka — high praise from a woman who grew up there. The tonkotsu is rich without being heavy. We are regulars now.
The spicy miso ramen here is legitimately great. Thick noodles, perfectly soft egg, solid broth. Seating is tight but worth the squeeze.
I live on Division so I've been coming here since they opened. Consistently excellent. The shoyu broth is my go-to on cold Portland nights.

Quick lunch after a long board meeting downtown — grabbed a salmon rice bowl from Sakura Cart and it was exactly what I needed. Fresh, generous portions, incredibly affordable. This cart is a gem in the Alder pod.
Great rice bowls for the price. The teriyaki chicken is tender and the portions are huge. Get there by noon or you'll wait.

Scouting venues for Mika's coming-of-age dinner and we did a trial run at Miyamoto. The omakase counter is stunning. Chef Miyamoto is meticulous — every piece of nigiri feels like a small ceremony. This will be the place for her celebration.
Without question the best sushi in Portland. The fish is incredibly fresh and the chef takes genuine pride in each piece. Pricey but completely worth it for a special occasion.
Really excellent nigiri and a calm, beautiful atmosphere. Service was attentive without being intrusive. Reservations are essential.

Toured The Pearl Event Center as a possible venue for our PAPEA annual gala. The main hall holds 200 comfortably, AV setup is modern, and the catering coordinator was responsive and professional. The price point is steep but the space is beautiful.
We held our nonprofit fundraiser here and could not have been happier. The staff handled setup and breakdown seamlessly and the room looked stunning. Highly recommend for any large formal event.

Stopped by after a long afternoon of school visits in SE Portland. The happy hour izakaya menu is a steal — edamame, gyoza, and a cold Sapporo for under fifteen dollars. Good energy and friendly bartenders.
Solid neighborhood izakaya with a laid-back vibe. The chicken karaage and the cold tofu are my regular order. Prices are very reasonable for the quality.

The okonomiyaki from this cart is the real deal — crispy edges, fluffy interior, properly topped with bonito flakes and kewpie mayo. I come to this pod just for Otafuku. Karen from my office started coming too and now we do a regular Friday lunch here.
Best Japanese street food cart in the city. The takoyaki is perfectly crispy outside and creamy inside. The owner is super friendly and takes real pride in the food.

We used Zen Garden Banquet Hall for Mika's coming-of-age celebration and it was perfect. The tatami-inspired decor added so much warmth, the catering was excellent, and the staff made every guest feel welcome. Such a meaningful evening — exactly what we hoped for.
Attended a private party here and was really impressed. The space has a serene, elegant feel without being stuffy. Parking can be tricky but the venue itself is wonderful.

Kaito on Mississippi is my Friday treat after a tough week. The black garlic tonkotsu is outstanding — deep, complex broth, great noodle texture. The tables are communal which I actually love. Great neighborhood energy.
Really good ramen in a fun, casual atmosphere. The vegan option with miso broth is surprisingly hearty. Staff are welcoming and the playlist is always great.

Kikuya is my go-to for a proper sit-down Japanese dinner. The sashimi is consistently fresh, the miso soup comes in a beautiful lacquer bowl, and the service strikes exactly the right tone — attentive without hovering. Michelle from the curriculum team and I come here after late planning sessions.
One of the few restaurants in Portland where the kaiseki-style courses are done with real care. The space is quiet and refined. Perfect for a business dinner or a special date night.

A solid handroll bar concept executed well. The salmon and cucumber roll is clean and fresh, and I appreciate that they let you watch the prep. Quick, casual, good for lunch or an early dinner before a show.

Visited Obon Community Hall as a potential PAPEA community event space. Modest but warm, with a real sense of history. The coordinator was incredibly helpful and flexible on pricing for nonprofit events. This could be a great fit for our smaller workshops.

Matcha House on 23rd is my weekend ritual. The ceremonial matcha latte is properly prepared — no artificial sweetness, just clean, grassy flavor. They also carry wagashi and the red bean mochi is wonderful with tea. I sent the whole district office staff here.
A little oasis of calm in the NW 23rd corridor. The hojicha soft serve is seasonal and incredible. Staff are knowledgeable about Japanese tea traditions and happy to explain the menu. A gem.

The takoyaki from Fuji Cart on SW Alder is the best in the city. Golden, piping hot, generously filled with octopus. Great value and the line is usually short. I grab these after board meetings when I'm too tired to cook.

Akebono does great yakitori — the tsukune chicken meatball skewer is my standout pick. Cozy, a little smoky (in the best way), and the craft beer list pairs nicely with the grilled items. Good spot for a low-key weeknight dinner.

Ichiban is the dependable lunch spot near the convention center. The bento sets are well-priced, the miso comes out hot, and service is brisk during the lunch rush. Not flashy but consistently good — I've been coming here for years.

Harbor Room has one of the best views in downtown Portland — the waterfront light at sunset during our event was genuinely stunning. Event staff were professional and the layout works well for cocktail receptions. Worth considering for PAPEA's larger community events.
